mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the long-term trend rate reduces equity risk premiums by confirming economic expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate signals tightening labor markets that boost corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite falling unemployment, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in industrial production and housing starts indicates synchronized supply-side capacity expansion that supports corporate margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict 60-day forward returns with a Rank IC below 0.05, or individual series ICs explain > 90% of the joint signal's predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates combined with accelerating inflation compress equity valuations by increasing the discount rate applied to future cash flows, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral during sustained rate hikes and inflation acceleration, or the combined signal's predictive power vanishes after controlling for market beta.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls sustains consumer spending power, which underpins corporate revenue forecasts and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in high-payroll-growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in retail and food services sales directly signals weakening consumer demand, which compresses corporate earnings guidance and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite falling sales, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing real GDP growth and rising consumer prices indicates stagflation, which erodes real corporate margins and forces equity de-rating, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict 20-day forward returns with a Rank IC below 0.05, or individual series ICs explain > 90% of the joint signal's predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production exceeding its historical baseline confirms robust manufacturing capacity utilization, which supports industrial and cyclical equity valuations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain negative or neutral when industrial production sustains levels above 100, or the signal's predictive power vanishes after controlling for market beta.
